zh.lifeSummary: 當William Collett於1454年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾武坡 斯勞特時，他的父親John Collett22歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Maguire22歲。他於大約1475年與Sadie O'Mally在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾蕾托 吉丁結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1509年在他的家鄉享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾武坡 斯勞特聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Collett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a pet form of the personal name Col(l), a pet form of Nicholas . There was also a feminine form, Collette, which may also have given rise to a surname. The name could also mean ‘Coll the younger’.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Collett was born in 1454, in Upper Slaughter, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Collett, was 22 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Maguire, was 22. He married Sadie O'Mally about 1475, in Little Gidding, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1509, in his hometown, at the age of 55, and was buried in St Peter's Church, Upper Slaughter, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: William Collett(이)가 1454년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 업퍼 슬로그h터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Collett(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Maguire(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1475년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 리틀 지딩에서 Sadie O'Mally(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1509년 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 업퍼 슬로그h터 스트 피터'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

ru.Saint Peter's Church, Upper Slaughter, C: ст питир'с чурч, уппир слотир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Collett родился в 1454 г. в местности уппир слотир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Collett было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Maguire, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Sadie O'Mally около 1475 г. в местности литл гиддинг, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1509 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности ст питир'с чурч, уппир слотир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
